devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । निशम्य देवः स्वभटोपवर्णितं प्रत्याह किं तानपि धर्मराजः । एवं हताज्ञो विहतान्मुरारेर्नैदेशिकैर्यस्य वशे जनोऽयम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrājovācaniśamya devaḥ svabhaṭopavarṇitaṃ pratyāha kiṃ tānapi dharmarājaḥevaṃ hatājño vihatānmurārernaideśikairyasya vaśe jano'yam
translation:
The King asked:
What did the god Dharmarāja (Yama, the presiding deity of righteousness), to whose authority this world is subject, and whose order was thus flouted by the servants of Viṣṇu (Murāri) reply to his servants, on hearing their report of that incident?

devanagari: यमस्य देवस्य न दण्डभङ्गः कुतश्चनर्षे श्रुतपूर्व आसीत् । एतन्मुने वृश्चति लोकसंशयं न हि त्वदन्य इति मे विनिश्चितम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: yamasya devasya na daṇḍabhaṅgaḥ kutaścanarṣe śrutapūrva āsītetanmune vṛścati lokasaṃśayaṃ na hi tvadanya iti me viniścitam
translation: Oh Sage! The quashing of the penalty or order of god Yama was never heard of at any time or from any person. It is my firm conviction, Oh Sage, that nobody else than you, can clear this doubt in the popular mind.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । भगवत्पुरुषै राजन्याम्याः प्रतिहतोद्यमाः । पतिं विज्ञापयामासुर्यमं संयमनीपतिम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācabhagavatpuruṣai rājanyāmyāḥ pratihatodyamāḥpatiṃ vijñāpayāmāsuryamaṃ saṃyamanīpatim
translation:
Śrī Śuka said (in reply):
Oh King I The servants of Yama whose attempts (to execute Yama’s order) were foiled by the messengers of Viṣṇu represented to their Lord Yama, the master of the city of Saṃyaminī.

devanagari: यमदूता ऊचुः । कति सन्तीह शास्तारो जीवलोकस्य वै प्रभो । त्रैविध्यं कुर्वतः कर्म फलाभिव्यक्तिहेतवः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: yamadūtā ūcuḥkati santīha śāstāro jīvalokasya vai prabhotraividhyaṃ kurvataḥ karma phalābhivyaktihetavaḥ
translation:
Yama’s messengers said:
Oh Lord I How many controllers are there of the world of living beings who perform three types of action (meritorious, sinful and mixed)? And how many are there who can dispense (lit. are the cause of the manifestation of) the fruits thereof?

devanagari: यदि स्युर्बहवो लोके शास्तारो दण्डधारिणः । कस्य स्यातां न वा कस्य मृत्युश्चामृतमेव वा ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: yadi syurbahavo loke śāstāro daṇḍadhāriṇaḥkasya syātāṃ na vā kasya mṛtyuścāmṛtameva vā
translation: If there be a number of rulers invested with the powers to dispense justice (lit. wielding the rod of punishment) who would (i.e. nobody would) suffer death, i.e. the result of sin leading to hell or enjoy nectar (i.e. the fruit of merit conferring celestial happiness) [in case these rulers of the world disagree], and who would not suffer both, i.e., hell and heaven, (in case they agree, one condoning an act and conferring heavenly bliss, and the other condemning the same act and dispensing suffering in hell, and both consenting to each other’s decision).

devanagari: किन्तु शास्तृबहुत्वे स्याद्बहूनामिह कर्मिणाम् । शास्तृत्वमुपचारो हि यथा मण्डलवर्तिनाम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: kintu śāstṛbahutve syādbahūnāmiha karmiṇāmśāstṛtvamupacāro hi yathā maṇḍalavartinām
translation: (It may be argued that there is no hitch, if several rulers dealing severally with the acts of these large numbers of beings are postulated. But in the case of postulating a plurality of rulers for dealing with vast numbers of beings engaged in multifarious activities, rulership will be (reduced to) nominal formality like that invested in tributary princes under an emperor (the real absolute ruler being different).

devanagari: अतस्त्वमेको भूतानां सेश्वराणामधीश्वरः । शास्ता दण्डधरो नॄणां शुभाशुभविवेचनः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: atastvameko bhūtānāṃ seśvarāṇāmadhīśvaraḥśāstā daṇḍadharo nṝṇāṃ śubhāśubhavivecanaḥ
translation: (Due to the untenability of plurality of controllers) you are, therefore, (according to us) the only one Supreme ruler of all beings including the gods. You are the sole controller and the judge to decide the good and evil actions of beings and the dispenser of reward and punishment (lit. wielḍer of the rod of justice to award heaven and hell).

devanagari: तस्य ते विहितो दण्डो न लोके वर्ततेऽधुना । चतुर्भिरद्भुतैः सिद्धैराज्ञा ते विप्रलम्भिता ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya te vihito daṇḍo na loke vartate'dhunācaturbhiradbhutaiḥ siddhairājñā te vipralambhitā
translation: Such as you are, your authority (lit. awarding of punishment) is null and void and has ceased to exist in the world today; for your order has been superseded (lit. tricked off) by four wonderful Siddhas (demi-gods).

devanagari: नीयमानं तवादेशादस्माभिर्यातनागृहान् । व्यामोचयन्पातकिनं छित्त्वा पाशान्प्रसह्य ते ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: nīyamānaṃ tavādeśādasmābhiryātanāgṛhānvyāmocayanpātakinaṃ chittvā pāśānprasahya te
translation: While, in compliance of your order, a sinner was being taken by us to the torture-houses (infernal regions), they perforce cut asunder your nooses (fastening the sinner) and liberated him.

devanagari: तांस्ते वेदितुमिच्छामो यदि नो मन्यसे क्षमम् । नारायणेत्यभिहिते मा भैरित्याययुर्द्रुतम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃste veditumicchāmo yadi no manyase kṣamamnārāyaṇetyabhihite mā bhairityāyayurdrutam
translation: As soon as the word ‘Nārāyaṇa’ was uttered (by the sinner), they hastened in saying, “Do not be afraid”. So we wish to know from you who they are, if you think it is in our interest to know them (otherwise by showing disrespect to them through ignorance, there may be a trouble to you).

devanagari: श्रीबादरायणिरुवाच । इति देवः स आपृष्टः प्रजासंयमनो यमः । प्रीतः स्वदूतान्प्रत्याह स्मरन्पादाम्बुजं हरेः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībādarāyaṇiruvācaiti devaḥ sa āpṛṣṭaḥ prajāsaṃyamano yamaḥprītaḥ svadūtānpratyāha smaranpādāmbujaṃ hareḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Thus enquired, god Yama, the controller of all created beings was pleased, and calling upon mentally, the lotus-like feet of Hari, replied to his messengers.

devanagari: यम उवाच । परो मदन्यो जगतस्तस्थुषश्च ओतं प्रोतं पटवद्यत्र विश्वम् । यदंशतोऽस्य स्थितिजन्मनाशा नस्योतवद्यस्य वशे च लोकः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: yama uvācaparo madanyo jagatastasthuṣaśca otaṃ protaṃ paṭavadyatra viśvamyadaṃśato'sya sthitijanmanāśā nasyotavadyasya vaśe ca lokaḥ
translation:
Yama said:
Quite different from me is the Supreme Ruler of the mobile and immobile creation. (Who transcends it. As his servant, I control the mobile beings, especially the human beings among them and more especially the sinners among men, while he is the Supreme Ruler of all). Into him the universe lies woven warp and woof, like a piece of cloth. It is from his parts (aṃśas viz, gods Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Rudra) that the creation, sustenance and destruction of the universe take place. The whole world remains under his perfect control like a bullock controlled by a string pierced through its nostrils.

devanagari: यो नामभिर्वाचि जनं निजायां बध्नाति तन्त्र्यामिव दामभिर्गाः । यस्मै बलिं त इमे नामकर्म निबन्धबद्धाश्चकिता वहन्ति ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: yo nāmabhirvāci janaṃ nijāyāṃ badhnāti tantryāmiva dāmabhirgāḥyasmai baliṃ ta ime nāmakarma nibandhabaddhāścakitā vahanti
translation: Just as one keeps bullocks tethered together to a long rope, he binds people to his word (viz. the Vedas) by designating them (into classes as Brāhmaṇas, Kṣatriyas etc.) and prescribing to each his respective duties. These people who are thus tightly regulated by their denominations and duties are afraid of him and bear him worship (by scrupulous performance of their prescribed work).

devanagari: अहं महेन्द्रो निरृतिः प्रचेताः सोमोऽग्निरीशः पवनो विरिञ्चिः । आदित्यविश्वे वसवोऽथ साध्या मरुद्गणा रुद्रगणाः ससिद्धाः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ mahendro nirṛtiḥ pracetāḥ somo'gnirīśaḥ pavano viriñciḥādityaviśve vasavo'tha sādhyā marudgaṇā rudragaṇāḥ sasiddhāḥ
translation: I (and the guardian deities of other cardinal points like) the great Indra, Nirṛti, Varuṇa, the Moon-god, the Fire-god, God Śiva, the Wind-god, as also the Sun-god, Brahmā, the (twelve) sons of Aditi (the monthly rotational solar presidents during a year), the Viśvedevas, the (eight) Vasus, demi-gods called Sādhyas, the (forty-nine) Wind gods, the group of (eleven) Rudras along with the Siddha tribe of demi-gods, and other progenitors of the universe (like Marīci), rulers of gods (i.e. ruler-like highly respected persons like Bṛhaspati), Bhṛgu and others who are not tainted with rajas and tamas—all these though dominated by the attribute sattva but are touched (deluded) with his Māyā do not understand his intentions—What of others? (Others can never fathom, it).

devanagari: अन्ये च ये विश्वसृजोऽमरेशा भृग्वादयोऽस्पृष्टरजस्तमस्काः । यस्येहितं न विदुः स्पृष्टमायाः सत्त्वप्रधाना अपि किं ततोऽन्ये ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: anye ca ye viśvasṛjo'mareśā bhṛgvādayo'spṛṣṭarajastamaskāḥyasyehitaṃ na viduḥ spṛṣṭamāyāḥ sattvapradhānā api kiṃ tato'nye
translation: I (and the guardian deities of other cardinal points like) the great Indra, Nirṛti, Varuṇa, the Moon-god, the Fire-god, God Śiva, the Wind-god, as also the Sun-god, Brahmā, the (twelve) sons of Aditi (the monthly rotational solar presidents during a year), the Viśvedevas, the (eight) Vasus, demi-gods called Sādhyas, the (forty-nine) Wind gods, the group of (eleven) Rudras along with the Siddha tribe of demi-gods, and other progenitors of the universe (like Marīci), rulers of gods (i.e. ruler-like highly respected persons like Bṛhaspati), Bhṛgu and others who are not tainted with rajas and tamas—all these though dominated by the attribute sattva but are touched (deluded) with his Māyā do not understand his intentions—What of others? (Others can never fathom, it).

devanagari: यं वै न गोभिर्मनसासुभिर्वा हृदा गिरा वासुभृतो विचक्षते । आत्मानमन्तर्हृदि सन्तमात्मनां चक्षुर्यथैवाकृतयस्ततः परम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: yaṃ vai na gobhirmanasāsubhirvā hṛdā girā vāsubhṛto vicakṣateātmānamantarhṛdi santamātmanāṃ cakṣuryathaivākṛtayastataḥ param
translation: Jīvas (individuals) verily cannot visualize him or comprehend him by means of their cognitive senses, mental faculties or vital breaths (which actuate the conative organs) or by means of speech (or Śāstras composed of words) even though he exists within their hearts and pervades them all (as the inner controller), even as the forms (which are the objects of the sight) cannot visualize the faculty of vision (which reveals them). He is beyond them all.

devanagari: तस्यात्मतन्त्रस्य हरेरधीशितुः परस्य मायाधिपतेर्महात्मनः । प्रायेण दूता इह वै मनोहराश्चरन्ति तद्रूपगुणस्वभावाः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyātmatantrasya hareradhīśituḥ parasya māyādhipatermahātmanaḥprāyeṇa dūtā iha vai manoharāścaranti tadrūpaguṇasvabhāvāḥ
translation: Generally, the messengers of Hari, the Supreme Soul, the self-dependent Supreme Ruler, the transcendent Lord of the Māyā go about in this world. They have attractive personalities and the same form, attributes and disposition as of their Lord.

devanagari: भूतानि विष्णोः सुरपूजितानि दुर्दर्शलिङ्गानि महाद्भुतानि । रक्षन्ति तद्भक्तिमतः परेभ्यो मत्तश्च मर्त्यानथ सर्वतश्च ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūtāni viṣṇoḥ surapūjitāni durdarśaliṅgāni mahādbhutānirakṣanti tadbhaktimataḥ parebhyo mattaśca martyānatha sarvataśca
translation: The messengers of Viṣṇu are worshipped by gods. Their most wonderful forms cannot be easily perceived. They protect the mortal devotees of Viṣṇu from enemies, from me and from all (sides and people) as well.

devanagari: धर्मं तु साक्षाद्भगवत्प्रणीतं न वै विदुरृषयो नापि देवाः । न सिद्धमुख्या असुरा मनुष्याः कुतो नु विद्याधरचारणादयः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: dharmaṃ tu sākṣādbhagavatpraṇītaṃ na vai vidurṛṣayo nāpi devāḥna siddhamukhyā asurā manuṣyāḥ kuto nu vidyādharacāraṇādayaḥ
translation: The (secret of the) doctrine as enunciated by the Lord himself could not be comprehended by (persons dominated by the attribute Sattva like the sages, gods and prominent Siddhas. How can it be understood by demons and men—much less by demi-gods like Vidyādharas and Cāraṇas.

devanagari: स्वयम्भूर्नारदः शम्भुः कुमारः कपिलो मनुः । प्रह्लादो जनको भीष्मो बलिर्वैयासकिर्वयम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: svayambhūrnāradaḥ śambhuḥ kumāraḥ kapilo manuḥprahlādo janako bhīṣmo balirvaiyāsakirvayam
translation: We twelve—god Brahmā, (the heavenly sage) Nārada, god Śiva, Sanatkumāra, Kapila (the founder of the Sāṅkhya doctrine), Manu, Prahlāda, Janaka, Bhīṣma, the demon Bali, Śuka (the son of Vyāsa) and we ourselves—know the essence of the Dharma (the esoteric doctrine) as expounded by the Lord. It is extremely sacred, pure, secret and difficult to comprehend. One who knows it attains immortality (Final Liberation—Mokṣa).

devanagari: द्वादशैते विजानीमो धर्मं भागवतं भटाः । गुह्यं विशुद्धं दुर्बोधं यं ज्ञात्वामृतमश्नुते ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: dvādaśaite vijānīmo dharmaṃ bhāgavataṃ bhaṭāḥguhyaṃ viśuddhaṃ durbodhaṃ yaṃ jñātvāmṛtamaśnute
translation: We twelve—god Brahmā, (the heavenly sage) Nārada, god Śiva, Sanatkumāra, Kapila (the founder of the Sāṅkhya doctrine), Manu, Prahlāda, Janaka, Bhīṣma, the demon Bali, Śuka (the son of Vyāsa) and we ourselves—know the essence of the Dharma (the esoteric doctrine) as expounded by the Lord. It is extremely sacred, pure, secret and difficult to comprehend. One who knows it attains immortality (Final Liberation—Mokṣa).

devanagari: एतावानेव लोकेऽस्मिन्पुंसां धर्मः परः स्मृतः । भक्तियोगो भगवति तन्नामग्रहणादिभिः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: etāvāneva loke'sminpuṃsāṃ dharmaḥ paraḥ smṛtaḥbhaktiyogo bhagavati tannāmagrahaṇādibhiḥ
translation: That alone has been remembered (prescribed in the Dharma Śāstra) as the highest Dharma (sacred duty) of all men in this world, viz., that they should cultivate.devotion unto the Lord through the utterance of his name (and other forms of bhakti).

devanagari: नामोच्चारणमाहात्म्यं हरेः पश्यत पुत्रकाः । अजामिलोऽपि येनैव मृत्युपाशादमुच्यत ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: nāmoccāraṇamāhātmyaṃ hareḥ paśyata putrakāḥajāmilo'pi yenaiva mṛtyupāśādamucyata
translation: Look at the great efficacy of the utterance of Hari’s name, my boys. It is due to that, that even (a great sinner like) Ajāmila was rescued from the clutches (lit. noose) of death.

devanagari: एतावतालमघनिर्हरणाय पुंसां । सङ्कीर्तनं भगवतो गुणकर्मनाम्नाम् । विक्रुश्य पुत्रमघवान्यदजामिलोऽपि । नारायणेति म्रियमाण इयाय मुक्तिम् ॥ २४ ...
english_devnagari: etāvatālamaghanirharaṇāya puṃsāṃsaṅkīrtanaṃ bhagavato guṇakarmanāmnāmvikruśya putramaghavānyadajāmilo'pinārāyaṇeti mriyamāṇa iyāya muktim
translation: That even a great sinner like Ajāmila, while on the point of death (when one’s faculties could not be concentrated on the Lord), simply shrieked out (and not devotionally chanted) for his son (and not for Lord Viṣṇu) as ‘Nārāyaṇa’ and attained Liberation shows that (mere utterance of Lord’s name—nāmābhāsa—is enough to annihilate all sins and that glorification of the Lord’s attributes and deeds and chanting of the Lord’s name—all these are not required for mere destruction of the sins (but they are needed for cleanly wiping out the impressions (vāsanās) left over by sins.

devanagari: प्रायेण वेद तदिदं न महाजनोऽयं । देव्या विमोहितमतिर्बत माययालम् । त्रय्यां जडीकृतमतिर्मधुपुष्पितायां । वैतानिके महति कर्मणि युज्यमानः ॥ २...
english_devnagari: prāyeṇa veda tadidaṃ na mahājano'yaṃdevyā vimohitamatirbata māyayālamtrayyāṃ jaḍīkṛtamatirmadhupuṣpitāyāṃvaitānike mahati karmaṇi yujyamānaḥ
translation: (As this esoteric doctrine was grasped by only a dozen persons and remained a great secret to others, these great men) like Manu( most probably did not know that) the (efficacy of Lord’s name) is this (as described above). Alas! Their mind is sufficiently deluded by the goddess Māyā. Their intellect is focussed on (and entangled in) the three Veḍas promising in flowery language attractive fruits (of sacrifices and other rituals). And they engage themselves in great exhibitory performances of sacrificial acts. (The exhibitory attraction of sacrifices as contrasted with the chanting of the Lord’s name in a solitary place, or due to their lack of faith in the efficacy of the Lord’s name leads people to performance of sacrifices etc.

devanagari: एवं विमृश्य सुधियो भगवत्यनन्ते । सर्वात्मना विदधते खलु भावयोगम् । ते मे न दण्डमर्हन्त्यथ यद्यमीषां । स्यात्पातकं तदपि हन्त्युरुगायवादः ॥...
english_devnagari: evaṃ vimṛśya sudhiyo bhagavatyanantesarvātmanā vidadhate khalu bhāvayogamte me na daṇḍamarhantyatha yadyamīṣāṃsyātpātakaṃ tadapi hantyurugāyavādaḥ
translation: Having reasoned in this way, persons of good intelligence certainly perform with concentrated mind, acts (such as chanting of the Lord’s name etc.) conducive to the loving devotion to the Lord of infinite attributes. They do not deserve punishment at my hands (as they are sinless). Even if sin is committed (inadvertently) by them, it is destroyed by their singing the glory and chanting the name of the Lord (who is widely sung by the Vedas).

devanagari: ते देवसिद्धपरिगीतपवित्रगाथा । ये साधवः समदृशो भगवत्प्रपन्नाः । तान्नोपसीदत हरेर्गदयाभिगुप्तान् । नैषां वयं न च वयः प्रभवाम दण्डे ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: te devasiddhaparigītapavitragāthāye sādhavaḥ samadṛśo bhagavatprapannāḥtānnopasīdata harergadayābhiguptānnaiṣāṃ vayaṃ na ca vayaḥ prabhavāma daṇḍe
translation: Do not (even) approach those good souls who have resorted to the Lord and entertain equal outlook toward all and whose holy stories are (therefore) sung by gods and Siddhas. For they are protected by the mace of Hari and neither we nor the Time-spirit is competent to punish them.

devanagari: तानानयध्वमसतो विमुखान्मुकुन्द । पादारविन्दमकरन्दरसादजस्रम् । निष्किञ्चनैः परमहंसकुलैरसङ्गैर् । जुष्टाद्गृहे निरयवर्त्मनि बद्धतृष्णान् ॥ ...
english_devnagari: tānānayadhvamasato vimukhānmukundapādāravindamakarandarasādajasramniṣkiñcanaiḥ paramahaṃsakulairasaṅgairjuṣṭādgṛhe nirayavartmani baddhatṛṣṇān
translation: Bring (only) those wicked persons who are averse to the sweet fragrant honey of the lotus-feet of Mukunda (Lord Viṣṇu) which is incessently tasted by multitudes of Paramahaṃsas (ascetics of the highest order, or noble Swans) who possess nothing of their own and are expert connoisseurs of sweetness (of Lord’s lotus-feet). Bring also those who have set their hearts on their households, which is the way to hell.

devanagari: जिह्वा न वक्ति भगवद्गुणनामधेयं । चेतश्च न स्मरति तच्चरणारविन्दम् । कृष्णाय नो नमति यच्छिर एकदापि । तानानयध्वमसतोऽकृतविष्णुकृत्यान् ॥ २९ ...
english_devnagari: jihvā na vakti bhagavadguṇanāmadheyaṃcetaśca na smarati taccaraṇāravindamkṛṣṇāya no namati yacchira ekadāpitānānayadhvamasato'kṛtaviṣṇukṛtyān
translation: Bring those fellows (to me) whose tongue does not describe the excellent attributes of the Lord or utter his name; whose mind does not remember his lotus-like feet; whose head does not even once bow down to Lord Kṛṣṇa and who have rendered no service to Lord Viṣṇu.

devanagari: तत्क्षम्यतां स भगवान्पुरुषः पुराणो । नारायणः स्वपुरुषैर्यदसत्कृतं नः । स्वानामहो न विदुषां रचिताञ्जलीनां । क्षान्तिर्गरीयसि नमः पुरुषाय ...
english_devnagari: tatkṣamyatāṃ sa bhagavānpuruṣaḥ purāṇonārāyaṇaḥ svapuruṣairyadasatkṛtaṃ naḥsvānāmaho na viduṣāṃ racitāñjalīnāṃkṣāntirgarīyasi namaḥ puruṣāya bhūmne
translation: May the Venerable Lord Nārāyaṇa, the Ancient- Person, pardon me for the offence committed by my men who are his men. For his devotees who are ignorant, folding of palms in reverence to the great persons is the way of requesting forgiveness. I bow to the perfect Person.”

devanagari: तस्मात्सङ्कीर्तनं विष्णोर्जगन्मङ्गलमंहसाम् । महतामपि कौरव्य विद्ध्यैकान्तिकनिष्कृतम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmātsaṅkīrtanaṃ viṣṇorjaganmaṅgalamaṃhasāmmahatāmapi kauravya viddhyaikāntikaniṣkṛtam
translation: The singing of Viṣṇu’s name and glory is, therefore, auspicious and blissful to the whole world. Oh King of the Kuru race! Please understand this to be a thorough expiation of the greatest[1] of sins.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
mahatām—Or ‘Of great persons’. Translate: ‘It deserves to be observed by great persons for the complete expiation of their sins, Oh Parīkṣit.’

devanagari: शृण्वतां गृणतां वीर्याण्युद्दामानि हरेर्मुहुः । यथा सुजातया भक्त्या शुद्ध्येन्नात्मा व्रतादिभिः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: śṛṇvatāṃ gṛṇatāṃ vīryāṇyuddāmāni harermuhuḥyathā sujātayā bhaktyā śuddhyennātmā vratādibhiḥ
translation: The mind is not purified to that extent by observance of vows (e.g. fasting) as it does by the properly-developed devotion of those who constantly listen to and sing of the great exploits of Lord Viṣṇu.

devanagari: कृष्णाङ्घ्रिपद्ममधुलिण् न पुनर्विसृष्ट । मायागुणेषु रमते वृजिनावहेषु । अन्यस्तु कामहत आत्मरजः प्रमार्ष्टुम् । ईहेत कर्म यत एव रजः पुनः स...
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇāṅghripadmamadhuliṇ na punarvisṛṣṭamāyāguṇeṣu ramate vṛjināvaheṣuanyastu kāmahata ātmarajaḥ pramārṣṭumīheta karma yata eva rajaḥ punaḥ syāt
translation: He who has tasted the sweet honey in the lotus-like feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa does not find pleasure again in objects offered by Māyā which he has contemptuously discarded as leading to sin and miseries (of hell). But the other person who is ignorant about the blissfulness of devotion is overcome with passions, desires to wipe out the sin in his heart by performing another karma which in its turn leads to (the creation of) another sin (like the proverbial bath of an elephant which covers itself with dust after coming out of water.

devanagari: इत्थं स्वभर्तृगदितं भगवन्महित्वं । संस्मृत्य विस्मितधियो यमकिङ्करास्ते । नैवाच्युताश्रयजनं प्रतिशङ्कमाना । द्रष्टुं च बिभ्यति ततः प्रभृत...
english_devnagari: itthaṃ svabhartṛgaditaṃ bhagavanmahitvaṃsaṃsmṛtya vismitadhiyo yamakiṅkarāstenaivācyutāśrayajanaṃ pratiśaṅkamānādraṣṭuṃ ca bibhyati tataḥ prabhṛti sma rājan
translation: Oh King! The servants of Yama were not wonder- struck in the mind as they remembered (their realization by personal experience) the greatness of Lord Viṣṇu as described to them by their master (Yama). Being afraid of persons who have resorted to Viṣṇu (Acyuta) they do not dare even to look at them since that day onwards.

devanagari: इतिहासमिमं गुह्यं भगवान्कुम्भसम्भवः । कथयामास मलय आसीनो हरिमर्चयन् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: itihāsamimaṃ guhyaṃ bhagavānkumbhasambhavaḥkathayāmāsa malaya āsīno harimarcayan
translation:
The venerable Agastya (the sage born from a pitcher) who was seated (on the peak of) Malaya mountain, worshipping god Viṣṇu, narrated to me this secret historical legend.
[back to top]
